raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
For February 2026, total actual spend came in at **£10,286** against a budget of **£10,665**, producing a net favourable variance of **£379 (−3.6%)** at the full report level.
Beneath this headline, however, three line items show significant individual variances that require attention, with significant-line actuals of £2,553 against a budget of £2,300 — an adverse movement of **£253 (+11.0%)** across those lines.
- **Advertising & Marketing** overspent by **£667 (+66.7%)**, driven by a deliberate and MD-approved acceleration of the Q1 spring campaign into February, pulled forward from the original March plan.
- **Repairs & Maintenance** overspent by **£786 (+786.3%)**, attributable to an emergency HVAC failure in the server room on 14 February, with a CFO-approved invoice of £886.30 from FastAir Engineering.
- **Subscriptions** was underspent by **£1,200 (−100.0%)**, with zero actual spend recorded against a £1,200 budget. No supporting context was found across email, chat, or calendar sources; this line requires immediate management review to determine whether renewals have been cancelled, deferred, or are pending.
The two adverse variances were unplanned but formally approved in-period. The favourable Subscriptions movement requires verification before any budget relief is recognised.

LINE COMMENTARY

Advertising & Marketing | +£667 (+66.7%) | Budget: £1,000 | Actual: £1,667
Actual spend of £1,667 exceeded the February budget of £1,000 by **£667**. The overrun is explained by the deliberate pull-forward of the Q1 spring campaign from its originally planned March launch into February. Evidence across Slack, email, and calendar is consistent: the campaign team noted on 2 February that client approval had been received early and that the decision was made to capitalise on the timing. The agency was briefed on 1 February with a kick-off meeting scheduled for 5 February. The MD formally approved the budget reforecast and campaign acceleration on 1 February, citing a weather-sensitive launch window at the agency's recommendation. The total February commitment of approximately £1,600 broadly reconciles with the £1,667 actual. Budget for March is likely to be reduced correspondingly, though this should be confirmed in the forward reforecast.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-23T12:15:00Z - slack-advertising_&_marketing-broad - marketing-slack-2026-02-03: Campaign team — moving the Q2 spring campaign launch to this month. Client approval came in early, let's capitalise. Total Feb commitment will be ~£1,600 vs the £1,000 March plan. Approvals from MD signed off.
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:15:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-broad - gmail-2026-02-01-campaign-approval: MD approval for Q2 campaign acceleration. Spring campaign moved from March to February at agency recommendation — weather-sensitive launch window. Budget reforecast note attached.
- Calendar - 2024-11-23T22:15:00Z - calendar-advertising_&_marketing-broad - cal-2026-02-01: Marketing launch meeting — Spring campaign kick-off moved to 5 February. Agency briefed on 1 February per Slack decision.

Repairs Maintenance | +£786 (+786.3%) | Budget: £100 | Actual: £886
Actual spend of £886 exceeded the February budget of £100 by **£786**. The variance is attributable to an emergency HVAC failure in the server room overnight on 13/14 February. An emergency engineer was called, a quote of £886 was approved by the CFO on 14 February, and works were completed the same day. A formal invoice from FastAir Engineering for £886.30 (inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate) was received and CFO-approved on 14 February. The invoice amount reconciles closely with the actual figure recorded. This was an unplanned, reactive spend event rather than a discretionary overrun.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-repairs_maintenance-broad - ops-slack-2026-02-14: URGENT — HVAC unit in server room failed overnight. Building manager called emergency engineer. Quote came in at £886. CFO approved. Works completed today.
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-broad - gmail-2026-02-14-hvac-invoice: Invoice from FastAir Engineering — emergency HVAC repair 14 Feb 2026. £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate. CFO approved same day.

Subscriptions | −£1,200 (−100.0%) | Budget: £1,200 | Actual: £0
Actual spend of £0 represents a **£1,200 favourable variance** against the full £1,200 budget. No charges were recorded for the month. Searches across Slack, Gmail (broad and narrow), and calendar returned no results for this line item in February 2026. It is not possible to determine from available evidence whether subscriptions were cancelled, deferred to a later period, subject to a billing timing change, or simply not yet processed. **No context found — recommend review.** Management should confirm the status of all subscription renewals due in February and assess whether any budget relief is permanent or whether a catch-up charge is expected in March.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following line items showed variances below the significance threshold and were not subject to detailed investigation. No commentary has been prepared for these lines; they are listed for completeness.
- **Sales** | +£506 (+8.4%)
- **Purchases** | −£500 (−100.0%)
- **Audit & Accountancy Fees** | −£3 (−6.1%)
- **Bank Fees** | −£15 (−50.0%)
- **Cleaning** | −£1 (−0.8%)
- **Entertainment – 0%** | −£50 (−100.0%)
- **Entertainment – 100% Business** | +£13 (+0.0%)
- **General Expenses** | −£254 (−211.7%)
- **Light Power Heating** | +£13 (+14.9%)
- **Motor Vehicle Expenses** | −£71 (−54.6%)
- **Postage Freight Courier** | −£50 (−100.0%)
- **Printing Stationery** | −£20 (−100.0%)
- **Rent** | −£46 (−4.4%)
- **Telephone Internet** | −£6 (−13.3%)
- **Travel National** | −£150 (−100.0%)
> Note: Several of these lines show 100% variances (e.g. Purchases, Postage, Printing Stationery, Travel National) which may warrant a brief management check even if the absolute amounts are below the formal significance threshold.
